title: Executive Order 8225—Abolishing Customs Collection District Number 44 (Iowa); Extending Limits of Customs Collection District Number 39 (Chicago) To Include the State of Iowa; and Revoking the Designations of Des Moines, Iowa, Oklahoma City, Oklahoma, and Tulsa, Oklahoma, as Customs Ports of Entry

flags.justification: Exercises specific, existing statutory authority for routine customs administrative reorganization.
Four clear, specific, itemized organizational changes.
Multiple related actions (district abolition, territory reassignment, port-of-entry revocations) all coherently serve one purpose: customs field-organization streamlining.
An organizational and jurisdictional reassignment, not a compliance regime against private parties.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A permanent organizational restructuring, not a temporary-authority grant.
Routine administrative streamlining; no oversight mechanism is removed.
No compliance regime is created by this order.
A routine, bounded administrative reorganization with an explicit thirty-day transition period allowing affected importers and customs brokers to adjust.
An internal Customs Bureau reorganization; no other agency is implicated.
No coverage population or exemption structure is created.

notes: Validation batch (negative). Structural weight: 0/10 = 0.0%. A well-designed administrative reorganization with a sensible built-in transition period.
